### Post by Dafydd on 2006-11-27
Hello,
I've messed up my grub (i think by using the puppy linux grub install).

Anyway, I have 1) puppy linux on hda1 2) vector linux on hda2 3) ubuntu edgy on hda3 - with the home files on hda5


Somehow, my grub only works with hda2 (vector linux from which i'm writing this email).

What should I do? I've tried messing around...

here's my grub menu.lst

# GRUB configuration file '/boot/grub/menu.lst'.
# generated by 'grubconfig'.  Sun Nov 26 15:19:36 2006
#
# The backup copy of the MBR for drive '/dev/hda' is
# here '/boot/grub/mbr.hda.11147'.  You can restore it like this.
# dd if=/boot/grub/mbr.hda.11147 of=/dev/hda bs=512 count=1
#
# Start GRUB global section
#timeout 30
color light-gray/blue black/light-gray
# End GRUB global section
# Linux bootable partition config begins
  title Linux (on /dev/hda1)
  root (hd0,0)
  kernel /boot/vmlinuz root=/dev/hda1 ro vga=normal
# Linux bootable partition config ends
# Linux bootable partition config begins
  title Linux (on /dev/hda2)
  root (hd0,1)
  kernel /boot/vmlinuz root=/dev/hda2 ro vga=normal
# Linux bootable partition config ends
# Linux bootable partition config begins
  title Linux (on /dev/hda3)
  root (hd0,2)
  kernel /boot/vmlinuz root=/dev/hda3 ro vga=normal
# Linux bootable partition config ends
# Linux bootable partition config begins
  title Linux (on /dev/hda5)
  root (hd0,4)
  kernel /boot/vmlinuz root=/dev/hda5 ro vga=normal
# Linux bootable partition config ends
# Linux bootable initrd config begins
  title Linux initrd /mnt/hda5/boot/initrd.img-2.6.17-10-386 (on /dev/hda5)
  root (hd0,4)
  kernel /boot/vmlinuz root=/dev/hda5 ramdisk_size=16263 root=/dev/ram0 rw
  initrd /mnt/hda5/boot/initrd.img-2.6.17-10-386
# Linux bootable initrd config ends
title Install GRUB to floppy disk (on /dev/fd0)
pause Insert a formatted floppy disk and press enter.
root (hd0,4)
setup (fd0)
pause Press enter to continue.
title Install GRUB to Linux partition (on /dev/hda5)
root (hd0,4)
setup (hd0,4)
pause Press enter to continue.
title -     For help press 'c', then type: 'help'
root (hd0)
title -     For usage examples, type: 'cat /boot/grub/usage.txt'
root (hd0)

### Post by Bachstelze on 2006-11-27
That's because GRUB is using the same kernel (/boot/vmlinuz) to boot the three distros. Obviously, it will work only on one of them - the one corresponding to the kernel.

How did you setup your partitioning ? Did you create an unique /boot partition for the three distros ?

---

### Post by djsroknrol on 2006-11-27
Try super grub boot disc....It can solve a number of problems and it's a great tool to have..You can find it [HERE]("http://supergrub.forjamari.linex.org/")
